title: planchonella calcarea
intro: Planchonella calcarea, also known as the "White Beech" or "Beechwood," is a species of tree native to the rainforests of Australia and New Guinea, belonging to the Sapotaceae family. The medicinal properties of Planchonella calcarea include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and provide relief from arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Herbal preparations of Planchonella calcarea can be made by infusing its bark or leaves in water, or by using its extracts in capsule or tincture form.
